creator: gavin@FreeBSD.org
text:
Responsible Changed
From-To: freebsd-amd64->feedback

To submitter: this sounds strange, especially the bit where you 
say that symbols are substuituted in the apache headers.  This 
sounds like it could possibly be a hardware problem to me, 
especially as it has only recently started happening.  Can you 
run (say) Memtest86 overnight on the machine to see if that finds 
any issues?  If that finds nothing, can you please show an example 
of these corrupted headers, and also the differences seen between 
the image on disk and the corrupted image that is served? 
Lastly, you could try upgrading to 7.2 and seeing if that resolves 
your issue - especlially as 7.0-RELEASE is no longer supported.
